channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Suspects allegedly posing as firefighters in Palisades had fake fire engine
item.description: “The occupants claimed to be from the ‘Roaring River Fire Department’ in Oregon,” the release stated.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Content creators respond to TikTok's uncertain future in the US
item.description: TikTok content creators who make a living promoting their businesses and personal brands on the app were afraid that they would have to find new ways to engage consumers and make money as the app’s future in the United States remained uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio will meet with peers from India, Japan, Australia after expected confirmation, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration would begin formal international engagements this week when Marco Rubio — expected to be confirmed soon as secretary of state — meets with the foreign ministers of India, Japan and Australia, U.S. officials say.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will grip much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump promotes new meme coin before taking office on pro-crypto agenda
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has launched a new cryptocurrency token that is soaring in value – and potentially boosting his net worth – just before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, WGN 9

item.title: Bird flu found in a Georgia commercial flock for the 1st time amid the nationwide outbreak
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — For the first time since the 2022 countrywide outbreak, bird flu hit a poultry producer in Georgia, the nation's top state for chicken production.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Immigrants in Chicago and other US cities brace for expected Trump deportation arrests
item.description: Immigrants in Chicago and other U.S. cities have been preparing for immigration arrests since President-elect Donald Trump won the November election.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will take hold in much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coco Gauff writes 'RIP TikTok USA' on a TV camera at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — American tennis star Coco Gauff mourned the loss of TikTok's app back home, writing on a TV camera lens “RIP TikTok USA” and drawing a broken heart right after winning a match at the Australian Open to reach the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s inauguration performers include A-list country artists, rappers, bands
item.description: Donald Trump is bringing out the star power for his inauguration performances. The Trump-Vance Inaugural Committee announced the lineup for the 60th Presidential Inauguration Weekend earlier this week, which, according to the committee, features “some of our nation’s most iconic musicians.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As Donald Trump prepares to assume the presidency for a second time, he faces a news establishment on its heels — but not flat on its back. Last year's presidential campaign suggested that many traditional outlets are not depended upon, or trusted, nearly as much as they used to be.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: President Joe Biden has posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other Black civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 6 dramatic moments that shaped Biden’s presidency
item.description: President Biden’s single term in office will be marked by several moments that saw the White House take the spotlight. It began with the pandemic and a raucous State of the Union. Then there was the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan and his handling of wars in Ukraine and Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposal would ditch BMI for determining obesity
item.description: According to a September 2024 data brief from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), 40.3 percent of U.S. adults were considered obese between August 2021 and August 2023.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor defends private meeting with incoming President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City's Democratic mayor, who faces federal bribery charges, is defending his private meeting with President-elect Donald Trump as the Republican prepares to retake the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: (The Hill) — President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he is likely to give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mother of woman killed in Eaton Fire sues California utility
item.description: Multiple lawsuits have been filed against Southern California Edison this week by residents and business owners with destroyed property.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Florida hotel discriminated against Arab American group after Hamas' Oct. 7 attack against Israel, DOJ says
item.description: A Florida hotel owner reached an agreement with the DOJ, which accused the hotel of discrimination after it canceled a planned conference for an Arab American group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, NewsNation

item.title: First responders saved a moose that fell through lake ice in New York. Here's how they did it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bull moose that fell through lake ice in the Adirondack Mountains was saved by New York conservation officials in a laborious cold-water rescue. The moose fell through the ice around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man pleads guilty to the carjacking and killing of a New Mexico police officer
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A South Carolina man pleaded guilty Friday to charges that he gunned down a New Mexico state police officer who had stopped to help him, marking a turning point in a case that had been set for trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of woman's disappearance, murder previously charged with attempted murder, kidnapping
item.description: Fayetteville, North Carolina, authorities on Thursday arrested Tyrell Siermons, 30, in connection with the January disappearance and murder of 25-year-old Heather Williams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
